entry.title: Washington Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Grain deal signed in Turkey; Russia running out of ground-attack missiles
  • 5 countries hit hard by the grain crisis in Ukraine
  • Russia and Ukraine agree to release blockaded grain exports
  • U.N. court rejects Myanmar’s opposition to Rohingya genocide case
  • Prince George grins on beach in new photo released to mark 9th birthday
  • Stolen artifacts, some from 400s B.C., returned to Italy from New York
  • Russian and Israeli planes hit Syria in separate strikes, kill at least 10
  • Russia to ‘run out of steam’ soon in Ukraine: U.K. intelligence chief
  • A drought in Italy’s risotto heartland is killing the rice
  • Sri Lanka raids protest camp as ousted leader’s allies consolidate power
  • Police raid kills 18 in Rio, sparking outcry and accusations of abuse
  • Thousands of frogs are dying in Australia. Scientists aren’t sure why.
  • War narrows the many divides between east and west in Ukraine
  • The apocalyptic vision behind Putin’s ‘golden billion’ argument
  • Ukraine live briefing: Moscow, Kyiv reach deal on blocked grain, Turkey says; E.U. raises interest rates to tackle inflation
  • Russia’s moves in Africa problematic for U.S. interests, general agrees
  • Italy’s Mario Draghi resigns; new elections are set for September
  • The world’s oldest known male panda, beloved in Hong Kong, dies at 35
  • European Central Bank takes big step to address rising inflation fears
  • Ukraine, Russia sign U.N. deal to export grain on Black Sea
  • Brazil prosecutors charge suspects in murder of Amazon pair
  • Libya's west-based govt calls for stop to Tripoli clashes
  • Kenya court finds 3 policemen, 1 informant guilty of murder
  • France, Egypt pressed on missing backpacker as leader visits
  • Miami-Dade rejects sex-ed textbook in test of state’s anti-LGBTQ law
  • Teen with toy water gun in TikTok trend killed by off-duty jail officer, police say
  • ‘Run Hawley Run’: Video of Josh Hawley fleeing Jan. 6 rioters sparks memes
  • Pat Benatar won’t sing ‘Hit Me With Your Best Shot’ after mass shootings
  • She won the lottery. Then she shared her windfall with strangers.
  • Shonka Dukureh, blues singer and ‘Elvis’ actress, found dead at 44
  • Post Politics Now: Trump, Pence make dueling appearances in Arizona, as both eye 2024
  • Bannon ‘chose allegiance’ to Trump over the law, prosecutors argue
  • Michelle Childs confirmed to seat on D.C. Circuit
  • What we know — and don’t know — about what Trump did on Jan. 6
  • Frantic Secret Service radio traffic show how close Pence was to danger
  • ‘I don’t want to say the election’s over.’ Trump dug in after Jan. 6 attack.
  • Trump ‘chose not to act’ as mob terrorized the Capitol, panel shows
  • Biden’s bout with covid tests his return-to-normal strategy
  • Officers describe assault on Jan. 6 in front of accused attacker
  • Steve Bannon trial closing arguments set for Friday
  • Post Politics Now: ‘Folks, I’m doing great,’ Biden says after positive coronavirus test
  • Closing arguments in Steve Bannon’s trial set for Friday
  • Twitter posts surprise drop in revenue amid battle with Elon Musk
  • Why Amazon is buying a little-known medical provider for $3.9 billion
  • The FBI says it busted a big crypto Ponzi scam. It just got bigger.
  • The job market is beginning to show cracks
  • How Gen Z teens accidentally blew up the myth of the lazy millennial
  • Twitch turns popular charity and safety tools into built-in features
  • South Carolina bill outlaws websites that tell how to get an abortion
  • Amazon just bought my doctor’s office. That makes me very nervous.
  • Historical Howard County, Md., farm lists for $4 million
  • Political Emails May Be Spam, But Gmail Wants to Set Them Free
  • Prosecuting Trump Has Only Become More Urgent
  • Secret Service Must Come Clean on Missing Texts
  • The Fed Is Failing in Four Ways
  • Why China’s Developers Are Facing Mortgage Boycotts: QuickTake
  • The pro-life movement needs to move cautiously in purple states
  • China wants to ‘reduce misunderstanding’ with the U.S. It could start by talking.
  • 5 things we learned in the Jan. 6 committee’s stunning hearing
  • Lis Smith’s self-serving story about Andrew Cuomo
  • Why Europe is tougher than you thought
  • Girl Scouts should not sell a Maryland forest to developers
  • Youngkin’s appointments raise questions about his ‘moderate’ label
  • To stop gun violence, we need to fight systemic injustice
  • Readers critique The Post: This is no movie
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The Economy Putin Didn’t Actually Ruin
  • Germany Takes a Stake in Struggling Gas Provider Uniper

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • UK's Dover port declares 'critical incident' as travellers face hours-long wait

entry.title: Guardian: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tour de France 2022: Christophe Laporte ends French wait to win stage 19 – live!
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Kyiv and Moscow sign deal to allow grain exports from blockaded Black Sea ports – live
  • England v South Africa: second one-day cricket international delayed by rain – live!
  • Florida chef’s pink sauce videos stir up TikTok backlash
  • Protest march called in Belfast after deaths of 14 homeless people
  • Olaf Scholz announces bailout for Germany’s largest Russian gas importer
  • German tourists told to take colourful luggage to avoid airport delays
  • Why do the minority who haven’t had Covid account for most new infections?

  • Climate activists in Italy glue themselves to Botticelli painting

  • Russian sponsorship row overshadows opening of Salzburg festival
  • UK under pressure from Europe over deletion of abortion commitments
  • ‘Our love triangle was outrageous!’ Neighbours legends remember the good, the bad and the very silly indeed
  • With pastry, with feta and tomato, with bourbon and lime … 17 delicious ways to make the most of apricots
  • ‘Strap in and enjoy the ride’: behind the scenes at Jean Paul Gaultier’s wild fashion musical
  • The best recent translated fiction – review roundup
  • We're living in an age of permanent crisis – let's stop planning for a 'return to normal' | James Meadway
  • Ithaca: ‘We said: “Stop supporting racist bands”. Our Facebook page was flooded with Nazis’
  • Google to be banned in Ukraine’s occupied Donetsk and Luhansk regions
  • ‘There’s nothing else to give them’: Liverpool food banks confront rising hunger
  • A day in the life of Liverpool Airport: ‘There is relief – people are free, at last, to fly’

  • Dover port declares ‘critical incident’ as travellers face six-hour queues
  • Unless we act soon, this heatwave is just a taste of things to come | Andrea Dutton
  • Lady Gaga: The Chromatica Ball review – a spectacular show of high camp and insect cosplay
  • The real story of January 6 isn’t what Trump did – it’s what he didn’t | Moira Donegan
  • Barcelona sell another 15% of La Liga TV rights in push to boost finances
  • West Ham bid for Eintracht’s Kostic and have Leicester’s Luke Thomas on radar
  • Prince Harry’s case against Home Office can proceed, high court judge rules
  • Panama Papers whistleblower speaks out: ‘Politicians must act – now’
  • I’ve fought wildfires for decades. None of it prepared me for the UK infernos this week | Tim Green
  • Plan for Shinzo Abe state funeral faces growing opposition
  • Being ‘economical with the truth’ has a long history in British politics – but enough is enough | Simon Jenkins
  • England’s gritty win against Spain bodes well for Euro 2022 chances | Karen Carney
  • Chess: five-times champion Magnus Carlsen aims high after abdicating title
  • Gilberto Gil at 80: ‘Bolsonaro has a retrograde worldview, an opposition to any advance’
  • Weather tracker: record-breaking heat continues to scorch western Europe
  • Shericka Jackson goes supersonic as Asher-Smith takes world 200m bronze
  • No Republican senator supported a climate plan – where is the party on the issue?
  • House panel says Trump ‘chose not to act’ during attack on US Capitol
  • ‘We have travelled for a month to find grass’: climate crisis piles pressure on Senegal’s herders
  • Sri Lanka security forces raid protest camp as Ranil Wickremesinghe flexes muscles
  • Under-fire All Blacks coach Ian Foster emerges with Rugby New Zealand’s backing
  • Republican candidate for New York governor Lee Zeldin attacked at event
  • Elvis actor Shonka Dukureh dies one month after big screen debut
  • Magull and Popp strike to sink Austria and send Germany into semi-finals
  • Sweden limber up for Belgium with one eye on England in semi-finals
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Twitter blames Elon Musk and advertising slump for revenue miss

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-22 15:05PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Greater Bay Airlines’ first flight set to take off for Bangkok from Hong Kong on Saturday
  • Revealing evidence of foreign forces undermining Hong Kong’s national security is not needed, just as proof of coronavirus is not shown to require wearing of face masks: CY Leung
  • Hong Kong Observatory logs second hottest July day on record at 35.6 degrees Celsius and says heat to continue
  • Hong Kong protests: teens set to face jail time for rioting
  • More Hong Kong owners are selling homes at losses as emigration and rising interest rates turn city into buyers’ market
  • Hong Kong child abuse scandal: ex-carer jailed for about 7 months, becoming second person sentenced over case linked to charity group
  • Hong Kong to increase beds in public hospitals to 2,500 for Covid-19 patients, reschedule elective surgical operations to ease strain on healthcare system
  • Hong Kong customs confiscates cannabis worth HK$30 million, arrests suspected triad member
  • 33-year-old appointed Hong Kong’s deputy technology chief as government reveals latest line-up of undersecretaries, political assistants
  • Stay away from ‘destructive forces’ in industry, John Lee warns Hong Kong journalists at media awards ceremony
  • Two thirds of mainland customers in Greater Bay Area want to buy insurance products in Hong Kong, survey shows
  • As Hong Kong swelters, offices set thermostats higher to fight climate change, but allow staff to dress down (a little)
  • Hong Kong teenager who kept up studies despite cancer diagnosis among those honoured in Student of the Year award
  • How will Hong Kong respond to crises? Revamped home and youth affairs bureau promises volunteer network, emergency stockpiles for quick community action
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hospital’s data dashboard cuts patient discharge time by a third
  • China’s Pinduoduo quietly expands e-commerce operations in Hong Kong, wooing consumers with bargains and direct shipping
  • Covid-19 has only reinforced the need for better healthcare in Hong Kong. John Lee must ride the reform wave
  • China’s navy has quietly commissioned a new submarine for its eastern fleet
  • Chinese team tracks beavers with AI, making Xinjiang wildlife work easier
  • Hong Kong scientists work on replicating lunar soil to help China build on the moon
  • China says progress is being made in India border talks, but New Delhi warns of stalemate
  • China’s gaming industry insiders praise new policy document but remain cautious on licensing prospects
  • China on guard as European Central Bank, US Fed interest rate hikes threaten greater spillover effects
  • Chinese video-sharing platform Bilibili cracks down on malicious content, banned behaviour spread by virtual live-streamers
  • Nanjing punishes officials for temple honouring Japanese war criminals
  • The last giants are gone: Chinese paddlefish and Yangtze sturgeon declared extinct despite conservation efforts
  • Timeline: how Didi went from poster child for China tech to cautionary tale of the risks incurred by defying Beijing
  • China considers further easing quarantine rules as it seeks to reduce economic damage from Covid curbs
  • Chinese scientists have found a rice gene that can boost yield and shorten the growth cycle
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping wishes Joe Biden a speedy recovery from Covid-19
  • China’s focus on gang violence after Tangshan attack does little to protect women
  • Joe Biden schedules summit with African leaders as US renews push to invest in the continent
  • China reporter cyberbullied over Abe tears in reported suicide attempt
  • Ukraine war could deepen China-Russia military ties, Japan defence white paper says
  • Chinese companies to kick off global depositary receipt sales on SIX Swiss Exchange as regulator grants conditional approval for Keda Industrial
  • Australia is risking its own neck for the US in the South China Sea – has it stopped to ask why?
  • Indonesian leader Joko Widodo to meet Xi Jinping in China next week
  • To avoid China-Taiwan military conflict, send Xi clear message on consequences: British spy chief
  • Will South Korea’s Indo-Pacific strategy ‘imitate’ US to counter China’s regional influence?
  • Why China’s Gen Z see climate change as less important than their Western peers
  • Thai police search for missing monkeypox patient in Phuket
  • Indonesian law targeting ‘disturbing’ online posts harms free speech, data privacy: critics
  • South Korea restores military drills once reduced to help Trump – gets tough with North Korea, bolsters ties with US
  • Japan outlines concerns over increased China-Russia cooperation amid Ukraine war
  • Japan regulator says release of treated Fukushima water can go ahead, locals and China worried
  • Taxpayer-funded state funeral for Japan’s Shinzo Abe triggers protests
  • East Timor’s President Jose Ramos-Horta hopes Indonesia will aid ‘symbolic’ Asean entry in 2023
  • Sri Lanka: Xi offers new president Wickremesinghe support amid crisis
  • India elects Droupadi Murmu as president, its first from a tribal community
  • Thailand confirms first monkeypox infection on day WHO meets to decide global health emergency
  • ‘Beacon of hope’ as Ukraine, Russia sign deal to resume grain exports – 22 million tons stuck due to war
  • ‘My children are gone’, cries father as Russian air strike on Syria kills at least 7
  • Iran’s nukes ‘galloping ahead’– IAEA chief warns, unable to monitor programme
  • Biden and Trump: a tale of 2 presidents with Covid-19
  • ‘It’s a massacre’: police raid on Rio favela kills at least 18, sparking anger
  • January 6 panel lays out Donald Trump’s ‘187 minutes of inaction’ when he watched TV in dining room
  • Ukraine war must end to prevent nuclear ‘abyss’, Belarus strongman Lukashenko says
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky hopes to turn tide of war with Russia as US considers sending fighter jets
  • US records first polio case in almost a decade, and patient has developed paralysis
  • Vladimir Putin and Saudi crown prince talk oil, just days after Joe Biden’s Middle East trip
  • Beloved monarch butterflies now listed as endangered, one step closer to extinction
  • Italy to hold snap election after PM Mario Draghi quits
  • Shanghai bets on infrastructure, fixed-asset investment to claw back economic losses from two-month citywide lockdown
  • Russian bourse records US$11 million worth of trades in Alibaba, other Hong Kong-listed stocks in first trial month
  • Video: Heatwaves and wildfires engulf parts of US and western Europe
  • Video: Going underground to beat the summer heat in parts of China
  • How emerging market currencies are weathering US dollar surge
  • Hong Kong stocks cap best week in July as China pledges to stem housing rot, Alibaba and Meituan lead tech winners
  • ECB surprises with aggressive hike, first since 2011, to end the era of negative interest rates in eurozone
  • A fifth of MNCs in China rethink office space leasing plans amid tough Covid-19 curbs, while 4 in 100 cancel expansion
  • What does Hong Kong’s move to cut out lawyers in mortgage transactions mean for property buyers
  • Chinese Communist Party branch formed in HSBC onshore venture
  • Central banks forced to continue raising interest rates despite slowdown after months of dithering
  • How sunburn harms our skin, often years later, and how to avoid it: apply sunscreen – SPF 30 or SPF 50, depending on what you’re doing
  • How Hong Kong restaurant Haku’s interior design was inspired by its eclectic menu and music playlist
  • Attention, K-pop fans: girl group NewJeans announce their arrival with music video that’s an instant YouTube hit
  • International arts collaboration via Zoom a new model forged in the coronavirus pandemic; Hong Kong dance troupe is a beneficiary
  • Just 1 Day movie review: Charlene Choi, Wong Cho-lam play would-be lovers in a romantic trifle – Erica Li’s directing debut
  • An NFT collection of food or monsters? Artist says it’s both – he finds the ‘beasts’ in mirrored images of dishes, from dumplings to French toast
  • The best dumplings in Hong Kong, a Times Square go-to and a filmmaker’s pick for fried chicken
  • In Disney+ series Pistol, the Sex Pistols, UK punk rock pioneers, are back on TV 45 years on from God Save The Queen
  • His story is also the story of Hong Kong: Alan Chan, designer, on an East-meets-West career
  • 10 years after Psy’s Gangnam Style, has a K-pop song ever hit in the same way?
  • 12 male celebrities with bromances on and off screen: from Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart, and Leonardo DiCaprio and Tobey Maguire, to Ben Affleck and Matt Damon who are childhood buds
  • Why are residential resort properties booming? After Covid-19, homebuyers are looking to invest in real estate with five-star amenities in Hawaii, the Bahamas and Vietnam
  • On Her Watch: How new materials are revolutionising watchmaking, as Chanel, Rolex, Van Cleef & Arpels, Omega and more brands create exciting women’s timepieces with modern elements and techniques
  • Who is Lalit Modi, the businessman dating Sushmita Sen? The founder of the US$1 billion Indian Premier League just announced on Instagram that he’s in love … but had to flee India in 2010
  • 20 celebrity kids who are BFFs: from Kim Kardashian and Jessica Simpson’s daughters, to J. Lo and Ben Affleck’s teens and Beyoncé’s Blue Ivy and Apple Martin, these stars’ mini-mes are bonding
  • What did Elvis Presley and Caitlyn Jenner have in common? Meet Linda Thompson, the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll’s girlfriend after Priscilla, who married the Kardashians star – but was left out of the biopic
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
